articles.title: US-Iran attacks enter 10th day, denting Pakistan's ceasefire push
articles.description: The United States and Iran exchanged fresh attacks across the Gulf as Pakistan tried to revive failed ceasefire talks. The escalation has stalled key shipping routes, lifted oil prices and hardened Washington's public stance.

articles.title: Geopolitics biggest threat to FMCG: Wipro Consumer MD Kumar Chander
articles.description: Geopolitical tensions and rising crude oil prices are the primary concerns for India's consumer goods sector. Demand is improving, supported by quick commerce and premiumization trends in urban areas. Rural demand has also strengthened over the past six to nine months. Wipro Consumer acquired S Brands, expanding its Southeast Asian presence significantly. Input costs remain elevated, impacting margins despite some easing from recent peaks.

articles.title: Parliament Watch: FM on rupee, `16.1 lakh cr fuel taxes, India-US trade talks and more
articles.description: Rupee depreciation is influenced by rising crude oil prices and reduced capital account support. India monitors US trade policy changes and remains engaged with Washington after a recent trade deal. Single-brand retail FDI has seen a slump, while multi-brand retail FDI experienced an increase. The textile PLI scheme has attracted significant investment and generated numerous jobs. Fuel taxes have fetched the government substantial revenue over the past five years.

articles.title: Fueling Tensions: Oil Prices Surge Amid Middle East Conflict
articles.description: Oil prices surged by 2% reaching a five-week high amid escalating tensions in the Middle East between the U.S. and Iran. Brent and WTI futures experienced significant gains due to concerns over supply disruptions. Conflict in the region and threats to oil logistics are impacting global energy trade.
